schema_amendments.description: Array tracking derivation path for every constant or structural condition. principle_10_compliant true only when all entries anchored at live edges or IR axioms.
Boolean gate. Circularity detection: paths must terminate at (a) live edge from published AI layer or (b) IR rule applied to mathematical axiom.
IR-12 (Causal Precedence, DDE derivation class) added at HR-D8-0. Derives from C1 (DDE definition) via IR-4. Not imported from Suppes/Pearl. First use: DRP-8 Lemma 1 step L1-2.

paper_title: The τ-Grounding Theorem: Physical Derivation of the Upstream Measurement Principle for Delay-Differential Systems

scope_boundary.IN: DDE systems satisfying C1-C4 with independently measurable τ
OUT: Mathematical objects with no τ parameter
scope_boundary.scope_note: Hard boundary. Any claim extending beyond DDE systems C1-C4 is a build failure. DRP-1 (C-DRP.8) governs all out-of-scope cases.

inbound_edges.label: K structure: K = (P, O, M, B)
UMP: for non-vacuous K, O must be causally independent of P
ωτ = π/2 at Hopf criticality → β = 1/2
DDE scope conditions: C1 (τ>0), C2 (FRM form), C3 (τ measurable), C4 (sub-critical)

outbound_edges_register.label: Dual-Path Convergence: τ-grounding (DDE) + epistemological proof (general) both reach UMP

inference_rules.name: Definition Expansion
inference_rules.form: Expand defined term to its definiens

inference_rules.name: Statistical Inference
inference_rules.form: Sample statistic within threshold → population property

inference_rules.name: Parsimony
inference_rules.form: Prefer simpler explanation when empirically equivalent

inference_rules.name: RG Fixed-Point Identification
inference_rules.form: System at RG fixed point → universality class membership

inference_rules.name: Universality Class Identification
inference_rules.form: Same fixed-point structure → same critical exponents

inference_rules.name: Perturbation Stability
inference_rules.form: Fixed point stable under perturbation → exponents robust

inference_rules.name: Causal Precedence (DDE derivation class)
inference_rules.form: For C1-C4 DDE system: Φ_τ forward-only → O(t'≤t) ⊥ x(t+τ)
inference_rules.justification: Derives from C1 (τ>0 makes Φ_τ non-invertible) via IR-4 (definition expansion). Not imported from external causal framework. First formalised DRP-8 Phase 0, HR-D8-0.

claim_registry.label: DDE Forward Causal Structure
claim_registry.statement: In any DDE system satisfying C1-C4, the forward causal operator Φ_τ: x(t)→x(t+τ) is strictly forward in time. An observation class O drawn from any time index t'≤t cannot be a logical function of x(t+τ).

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS system S satisfying C1-C4 AND observation set O = {x(t_i): t_i ≤ t_ref} AND state x(t_ref+τ) SUCH THAT T_{x(t_ref+τ) → O} > δ_causal

WHERE.C2: Smooth C¹ right-hand side: ẋ(t) = f(x(t), x(t−τ)) with f ∈ C¹
WHERE.C3: Single fixed delay τ > 0, independently measurable via Protocol 3
WHERE.C4: Well-posed IVP on [−τ, 0]: unique solution exists for given history segment
WHERE.O: Countable indexed set drawn from causal past: t_i ≤ t_ref
WHERE.T_{x(t_ref+τ) → O}: Transfer entropy (Schreiber 2000): T_{X→Y} = H(Y_future | Y_past) − H(Y_future | Y_past, X_past). Measures directed causal influence of x(t_ref+τ) on O. Asymmetric: T_{X→Y} ≠ T_{Y→X} in general, unlike mutual information I(X;Y) which is symmetric and would be non-zero in any deterministic DDE regardless of causal direction.
WHERE.δ_causal: 3σ of background transfer entropy under time-shuffled surrogate null model (destroy temporal ordering of source while preserving marginal distribution)
WHERE.noise_model: Additive observational noise η ~ N(0, σ²_obs) with σ_obs > 0 required for transfer entropy estimation. Purely deterministic trajectories must be augmented with measurement noise floor ε ≥ instrument precision to ensure well-defined conditional entropies.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Specify C1-C4 system S and measure τ via Protocol 3. (2) Draw O from t_i ≤ t_ref. (3) Estimate T_{x(t_ref+τ) → O} using the KSG-based transfer entropy estimator (Kraskov et al. 2004, k=5, ≥500 paired samples; see Schreiber 2000 for TE definition). (4) Construct surrogate null: shuffle temporal indices of x(t_ref+τ) series to destroy causal coupling while preserving marginal statistics. Compute δ_causal as 3σ of surrogate TE distribution (≥200 surrogates). (5) Test T_{x(t_ref+τ) → O} > δ_causal.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: NOT FALSIFIED if T_{x(t_ref+τ) → O} ≤ δ_causal for all tested C1-C4 systems. Note: symmetric mutual information I(O; x(t_ref+τ)) is NOT a valid test statistic here because I > 0 is expected in any deterministic DDE (the future is a function of the past), making MI unable to discriminate causal direction.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP8.1 | Type F | DDE causal isolation | Transfer entropy (Schreiber 2000) via KSG estimator k=5 | replaces symmetric MI (v1) following Grok review identifying MI symmetry invalidates directional causal test | same KSG implementation as P3 substrate validation
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: C1-C4 system with backward-coupled noise injection where x(t_ref+τ) is directly fed into history. Produces T_{x(t_ref+τ) → O} > δ_causal. Witness deliberately violates causal structure to demonstrate predicate discriminating power. C6: PASS.

claim_registry.label: τ-Gap Measurement Window
claim_registry.statement: For any proposition P asserting R(x(t_P)) in a C1-C4 DDE system, observation class O satisfies causal independence from P's truth-maker if and only if O is drawn from W = {x(t_i): t_i ≤ t_P − τ}. The window boundary is determined exactly by τ.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS DDE system S satisfying C1-C4 AND proposition P asserting R(x(t_P)) AND observation class O_out = {x(t_i): t_i > t_P − τ + δ_boundary} AND K = (P, O_out, M, B) is non-vacuous

WHERE.non_vacuous: M can distinguish P-true from P-false worlds using O_out alone (C6 criterion)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Specify P, measure τ via C3. (2) Construct O_out with t_i > t_P − τ + δ_boundary. (3) Construct M. (4) Apply C6: can M return FALSIFIED on at least one possible O_out state? (5) If yes: K non-vacuous → predicate FALSIFIED.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: NOT FALSIFIED if every K constructed with O_out is vacuous. Systematic difference required: |measured_boundary − τ| > δ_boundary.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP8.2 | Type F | window boundary exactness | δ_boundary = 5% of τ | systematic deviation not noise
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: t_P = 10τ, O_out = {x(10τ + τ/2)}. Downstream observation within causal window. K non-vacuous if M can test R(x(t_P)) via downstream correlation. C6: PASS.

claim_registry.label: τ-Grounding Theorem
claim_registry.statement: For any DDE system satisfying C1-C4, the delay parameter τ is the common cause of: (a) the quarter-wave resonance ωτ=π/2 at Hopf criticality producing β=1/2 (frequency domain); and (b) the causal independence boundary at t_P−τ making W the necessary upstream measurement domain for non-vacuous DDE predicates (time domain). UMP for DDE propositions is entailed by the causal structure imposed by τ.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS DDE system S satisfying C1-C4 SUCH THAT β=1/2 is confirmed (ωτ=π/2, F-1.4 satisfied) AND |τ_boundary − τ| > δ_threshold

WHERE.τ_boundary: Empirically measured causal independence boundary: transition point in I({x(t_i): t_i≤t'}; x(t_P)) scanned as function of t'

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Confirm C1-C4. (2) Measure τ via Protocol 3. (3) Confirm β=1/2. (4) Measure τ_boundary using KSG estimator (k=5, ≥500 samples, same implementation as P3 substrate validation). (5) Compute |τ_boundary − τ|. FALSIFIED if > δ_threshold AND β=1/2 confirmed.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: NOT FALSIFIED if |τ_boundary − τ| ≤ δ_threshold for all tested C1-C4 systems with confirmed β=1/2. Systematic deviation required, not measurement noise.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP8.3 | Type F | main result | τ must drive both β=1/2 and causal boundary | δ_threshold = 5% of τ | KSG k=5 500+ samples
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: DDE with τ varied while k=π/(2τ) fixed so ωτ=π/2 always. If τ_boundary tracks τ: NOT FALSIFIED. If τ_boundary fixed while τ varies: FALSIFIED. Discriminating scenario exists. C6: PASS.

claim_registry.label: Scope Restriction
claim_registry.statement: The τ-Grounding Theorem (C-DRP8.3) applies exclusively to DDE systems satisfying C1-C4 with independently measurable τ. For all out-of-scope cases, DRP-1 C-DRP.8 is the sole derivation of UMP. C-DRP8.4 does not weaken C-DRP.8.
claim_registry.type_D_note: True by logical structure of C-DRP8.3 and scope conditions C1-C4. Not independently falsifiable — falsifying would require the causal argument to hold for τ=0 systems, which contradicts C1.

claim_registry.label: Dual-Path Convergence of UMP
claim_registry.statement: UMP is derived by two independent paths: Path A (DRP-1 §10.7, epistemological, general) and Path B (DRP-8 Lemmas 1-3, DDE causal physics, restricted). No step in Lemmas 1-3 logically requires DRP-1 §10.7 as a necessary premise.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS a step S_k in Lemmas 1-3 SUCH THAT S_k logically requires DRP-1 §10.7 as a necessary premise — i.e., S_k cannot be re-derived without §10.7 being true

WHERE.Lemmas_1_3: Steps L1-0 through L1-3, L2-0 through L2-4, L3-0 through L3-4 (14 steps) plus TH-1 through TH-CON (4 steps) = 18 steps total in D8-2.0
WHERE.DRP1_s10_7: The contradiction proof of UMP in DRP-1: if O=f(P) then M's output is determined by P's truth value making K vacuous
WHERE.necessary_premise: C is necessary for step S_k if removing C causes S_k's justification to fail

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Step-by-step trace of all steps in D8-2.0. For each step: (1) read justification field; (2) check whether it cites §10.7 contradiction argument as necessary premise; (3) if yes, verify step cannot be re-derived without §10.7. Finite (18 steps), deterministic, executable. Self-correction clause: if D8-2.0 record later found incomplete, re-run against corrected record.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: NOT FALSIFIED if all steps have no necessary premise from §10.7. The following do NOT constitute falsification: (a) receiving C-DRP.8 as Type A axiom; (b) both paths concluding UMP; (c) DRP-8 using K definition from DRP-1. DRS instance confirmed: 0/16 primary steps cite §10.7 as necessary premise (HR-D8-3-DRS, S55).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP8.CON | Type F | proof-theoretic claim | step-trace evaluation | DRS instance HR confirmed NOT FALSIFIED S55 | L3-4 amendment: C-DRP.8 in notes field only (convergence note, not premise) | TH-3 amendment: domain-specific intermediate steps
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: If step L3-0 were required to cite §10.7 as necessary premise (not just receive its conclusion) and L3 could not proceed without §10.7's proof method: FALSIFIED. Coherent scenario. C6: PASS.
falsification_predicate.drs_trace_result: HR-D8-3-DRS S55: 8 challenges, 6 DISMISSED, 2 PARTIALLY SUSTAINED (prose precision only). 0/18 steps require §10.7 as necessary premise. C-DRP8.CON: NOT FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Fifth Isomorphism Level
claim_registry.statement: The measurement independence condition is structurally isomorphic in both FRM physical and DRS epistemological domains because τ defines causal separation identically at both levels. This is a fifth isomorphism instance of the DRP-1 four-level framework.
claim_registry.type_D_note: True if and only if C-DRP8.3 is true. No independent test. DRP-1 v2.0 forward pointer: note fifth isomorphism level found in DRP-8.

placeholder_register.description: Stochastic DDE extension: whether τ-Grounding Theorem extends to SDDEs under probability-theoretic conditions. C1 assumes deterministic DDE. SDDE case deferred.
Distributed delay extension: whether W must be reformulated for DDEs with distributed delay kernels. C1 assumes single scalar τ.

pass_forward_register.label: Dual-Path Convergence: τ-grounding (DDE) + epistemological proof (general) both reach UMP independently

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: C1 axiom (DDE definition) — received from P1 AI layer v13 (live edge). Independently measurable by Protocol 3 (C3). Not stipulated.
F-1.4 live edge from P1/P2 PHASE-READY AI layers. ωτ=π/2 at Hopf criticality → β=1/2 (Hopf bifurcation analysis). Not stipulated.

3σ is a standard statistical threshold (IR-7). Inherited from P1/P3 falsification predicate conventions. Transfer entropy (Schreiber 2000) replaces symmetric MI as test statistic. Not free — anchored at statistical convention with justification in CONTEXT field.
5% threshold inherited from P3 C-3.REG measurement precision standard (live edge, P3 AI layer v2). Not stipulated — matches P3 substrate validation precision.
Derives from C1 (τ>0 makes Φ_τ non-invertible) via IR-4 (Definition Expansion). Derived rule, not axiom. Accepted at HR-D8-0.

principle_10_compliant_note: Set true at Phase 5 CBT Step 4. All 5 audit entries have derivation_path values anchored at live edges (P1, P2, P3 AI layers) or IR axioms. No free constants or stipulated conditions.

Registry update: 162+6=168 claims, 16 papers.
